plan: Step 1: Create `src/render/buildCathedralGeometry.ts` ‚Äî a pure geometry builder function `buildCathedralGeometry(params: SceneParams): THREE.Group`. This function creates a THREE.Group containing three sub-groups: (a) a base/platform using BoxGeometry scaled by height, (b) a nave (elongated BoxGeometry body) scaled by height and symmetry, and (c) two towers (CylinderGeometry or BoxGeometry) mirrored on the X-axis, with height driven by `params.height` and X-offset driven by `params.symmetry`. Only `height` and `symmetry` are consumed by this builder; `fracture` is deferred to US-012 (ruin modifiers), `fog` and `lightIntensity` are deferred to US-013 (atmosphere/lighting), and `ruinLevel` is deferred to US-012. Each deferred param should be documented in a code comment at the top of the file so the builder's contract is explicit and testable.

Step 2: Create `src/render/rebuildCathedral.ts` ‚Äî exports `rebuildCathedral(scene: THREE.Scene, currentGroup: THREE.Group | null, params: SceneParams): THREE.Group`. This function: (a) if `currentGroup` is non-null, traverses it, disposes all geometries and materials on every mesh, then removes it from the scene; (b) calls `buildCathedralGeometry(params)` to create a fresh group; (c) adds the new group to the scene and returns it. This separation keeps disposal logic testable independently from the builder.

Step 3: Create `src/render/sceneParamsChanged.ts` ‚Äî exports `sceneParamsChanged(a: SceneParams, b: SceneParams): boolean` that does a shallow equality check on all 6 fields. This is the dirty-flag guard that prevents per-frame rebuilds. Since `rebuildCathedral` does full dispose+recreate, calling it every frame would be catastrophic for performance. The animation loop will call `sceneParamsChanged` before triggering a rebuild, ensuring geometry is only recreated when SceneParams actually change.

Step 4: Extend `RendererHandle` in `initRenderer.ts` ‚Äî add `update(params: SceneParams): void` to the `RendererHandle` interface. This is an explicit interface extension of the US-004 API surface. The existing `dispose()` contract and all its tests must continue to pass unchanged. Inside `initRenderer`, maintain a `let cathedralGroup: THREE.Group | null = null` and a `let lastParams: SceneParams | null = null`. The `update()` method checks `sceneParamsChanged(lastParams, params)` before calling `rebuildCathedral`. Update `dispose()` to also dispose the cathedral group (traversing and disposing all child geometries/materials) in addition to the existing cleanup. The existing dispose-contract tests must not be adjusted to fit ‚Äî they are load-bearing and must pass as-is. New tests will cover the extended behavior additively.

Step 5: Replace the placeholder rotation animation. Remove `createPlaceholderMesh()` call and the `mesh.rotation.x += 0.005; mesh.rotation.y += 0.01` lines. Instead, apply a slow Y-axis rotation to the cathedral group (`cathedralGroup.rotation.y += 0.003`) in the animation loop when `!reducedMotion` and `cathedralGroup` is non-null. This provides visual feedback that the render loop is alive without the placeholder cube. If `update()` has never been called (cathedralGroup is null), render an empty scene ‚Äî the cathedral appears on first `update()` call. This avoids a blank screen regression since `main.ts` will call `update()` immediately after init.

Step 6: Mark `createPlaceholderMesh.ts` as deprecated with a `@deprecated` JSDoc comment and remove its import from `initRenderer.ts`. Do NOT delete the file yet ‚Äî it may serve as a fallback reference for other tickets or during integration. The intent is to delete it once US-011 is confirmed stable, but keeping it avoids breaking any transitive imports during the transition.

Step 7: Wire `RendererHandle.update()` into `main.ts`. In the aggregator subscription callback and the preset state subscription callback (where `mapSnapshotToScene` is already called), add `renderer.update(sceneParams)`. Also call `renderer.update()` with initial scene params right after init, so the cathedral is visible immediately on load without waiting for the first aggregator tick.
Step 8: Update `src/render/index.ts` barrel exports to include `buildCathedralGeometry`, `rebuildCathedral`, and `sceneParamsChanged` for testability.

Step 9: Write unit test `tests/unit/geometryBuilder.test.ts` ‚Äî tests for `buildCathedralGeometry`: (a) returns a THREE.Group, (b) group contains at least 3 children (base, nave, towers), (c) height=1/symmetry=1 produces taller geometry than height=0.2/symmetry=0.5, (d) rebuilding with different params produces a new group (not mutated), (e) all children are Mesh instances with geometry and material properties. Also test `sceneParamsChanged`: (a) returns false for identical params, (b) returns true when any single field differs.

Step 10: Write unit test `tests/unit/rebuildCathedral.test.ts` ‚Äî tests for `rebuildCathedral`: (a) disposes all geometries and materials on the old group before creating a new one, (b) removes old group from scene, (c) adds new group to scene, (d) returns the new group, (e) handles null currentGroup gracefully (first call). Also add tests verifying the extended RendererHandle: (f) `update()` exists on RendererHandle, (g) existing dispose-contract tests from US-004 continue to pass without modification.

Step 11: Write Playwright smoke test addition in `tests/e2e/renderer.spec.ts` ‚Äî add a test that verifies the canvas still renders non-black pixels after the cathedral geometry replaces the placeholder, confirming the visual pipeline is intact end-to-end.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test` to verify all existing unit tests pass unchanged, especially the US-004 dispose-contract tests in `tests/unit/initRenderer.test.ts` which must not be modified
Run `tests/unit/geometryBuilder.test.ts` to verify buildCathedralGeometry produces correct group structure, sceneParamsChanged guard works, and only height+symmetry affect geometry
Run `tests/unit/rebuildCathedral.test.ts` to verify dispose-before-rebuild cycle has zero leaked meshes and RendererHandle.update() integration works
Run `npm run test:e2e` to verify Playwright smoke tests pass ‚Äî canvas renders non-black pixels with cathedral geometry, preset switching triggers visual updates
Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck` to verify no type errors from the RendererHandle interface extension and all new code passes linting
Manually verify in browser (`npm run dev`) that: cathedral is visible on load, slow Y-rotation is active, switching presets changes geometry shape, and reduced-motion media query stops rotation
risks: RendererHandle interface extension (adding `update()`) is a public API change on top of US-004. Mitigated by keeping `dispose()` contract unchanged and testing existing tests pass unmodified.

Full dispose+recreate on every SceneParams change could cause frame drops if params change rapidly. Mitigated by the `sceneParamsChanged` shallow-equality guard in Step 3, ensuring rebuilds only happen on actual value changes.

Three.js mock complexity in tests ‚Äî the existing test file mocks many THREE constructors. New tests for Group traversal and child disposal will need additional mocks (Group with children array, traverse method). Keep mocks minimal and focused on dispose verification.

Removing placeholder mesh changes the visual output ‚Äî if `update()` is never called, the scene is empty. Mitigated by wiring `update()` with initial params immediately in main.ts (Step 7), and the Playwright smoke test (Step 11) catches regressions.
createPlaceholderMesh.ts left as deprecated dead code could confuse future contributors. Mitigated by explicit @deprecated annotation and stated intent to delete after US-011 stabilizes.
